subject: computer-science
description: Management Information Systems MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: What is a Management Information System (MIS)?
options: A planned system of collecting, storing and disseminating data in the form of information needed to carry out the functions of management
mcqs.explanation: MIS is a planned system of collecting, storing and disseminating data in the form of information needed to carry out the functions of management.

mcqs.q: How many key components does MIS comprise?
mcqs.explanation: MIS comprises four key components: People, Data, Software and Hardware.

mcqs.q: Which of the following are the four key components of MIS?
mcqs.explanation: The four key components of MIS are People, Data, Software and Hardware.

mcqs.q: MIS as an academic subject is the study of:
options: People, technology, organizations and the relationships among them
mcqs.explanation: Management Information Systems is the study of people, technology, organizations and the relationships among them.

mcqs.q: An Information System can be any organized combination of:
options: People, hardware, software, communications networks and data resources
mcqs.explanation: An Information System is any organized combination of people, hardware, software, communications networks and data resources that collects, transforms and disseminates information in an organisation.

mcqs.q: Workers who collect, store, manage and analyze data as their primary activity are called:
mcqs.explanation: Data workers are workers who collect, store, manage and analyze data as their primary activity, or as a relevant part of their activity.

mcqs.q: In MIS, an Entity is:
options: The real-world object, such as a person, place etc
mcqs.explanation: An Entity is a real-world object, such as a person, place, etc.

mcqs.q: Meta-Data refers to:
mcqs.explanation: Meta-Data is the information about data — describing structure, format, source etc.

mcqs.q: In MIS, a Prototype is:
options: A final version of the software
A working model of an existing system
mcqs.explanation: A Prototype is a working model of an existing system.

mcqs.q: Integration in system concepts refers to:
options: The holism of the system in real time
mcqs.explanation: Integration refers to the holism of the system in real time — the system as a unified whole.

mcqs.q: Groupware is the popular term for software that:
options: Supports the collaborative efforts of a team
mcqs.explanation: Groupware is the popular term for the software component that supports the collaborative efforts of a team.

mcqs.q: A Feedback System is concerned with:
options: The way information is used for the purpose of control
mcqs.explanation: A Feedback System is concerned with the way information is used for purpose of control and applies technologies.

mcqs.q: A Deterministic System operates in a:
mcqs.explanation: A Deterministic System operates in a predictable manner — given the same input it always produces the same output.

mcqs.q: What is the most rapidly growing application for computer-assisted decision making?
mcqs.explanation: Simulation is the most rapidly growing application for computer-assisted decision making.

mcqs.q: Which type of system monitors the elementary activities and transactions of the organisation?
mcqs.explanation: Operational-Level Systems monitor the elementary activities and transactions of the organisation — keeping track of daily regular business transactions.

mcqs.q: Which system transforms enterprise data into quickly accessible executive-level reports?
mcqs.explanation: ESS (Executive Support System) is software that allows users to transform enterprise data into quickly accessible executive-level reports.

mcqs.q: Projections and responses to queries are information output characteristics associated with:
mcqs.explanation: Projections and responses to queries are information output characteristics associated with ESS (Executive Support Systems).

mcqs.q: From which system does an MIS receive its input?
mcqs.explanation: MIS receives input from the TPS (Transaction Process System) — the system that captures all business transactions.

mcqs.q: MIS usually serves managers interested in:
mcqs.explanation: MIS usually serves managers interested in weekly, monthly and yearly results — not day-to-day activities.

mcqs.q: What does TPS stand for in MIS?
mcqs.explanation: TPS stands for Transaction Process System — an information processing system for business transactions involving collection, modification and retrieval of all transaction data.

mcqs.q: Which system supports structured and semi-structured decision making?
mcqs.explanation: DSS (Decision Support System) supports structured and semi-structured decision making.

mcqs.q: EIS (Executive Information System) is best described as:
options: A combination of MIS and DSS
mcqs.explanation: EIS (Executive Information System) is a combination of a MIS and DSS.

mcqs.q: GIS in the context of information systems stands for:
mcqs.explanation: GIS stands for Geographical Information System — a computer-based system that stores and manipulates data viewed from a geographical reference.

mcqs.q: Which type of system deals with the projections of the future?
mcqs.explanation: Strategic Planning Systems deal with the projections of the future.

mcqs.q: A Personnel Information System deals with:
options: The flow of information about people working in the organization
mcqs.explanation: A Personnel Information System deals with the flow of information about people working in the organization.

mcqs.q: The effectiveness of a Marketing Information System depends largely on:
options: Feedback from the marketplace to the firm
mcqs.explanation: The effectiveness of the Marketing Information System depends to a larger extent on feedback from the marketplace to the firm.

mcqs.q: Identifying customers and markets using demographics, consumer behaviour and trends is an example of which type of system?
options: Knowledge-level sales and marketing information system
mcqs.explanation: Identifying customers and markets using data on demographics, markets, consumer behaviour and trends is an example of a knowledge-level sales and marketing information system.

mcqs.q: Operational-Level Systems primarily keep track of:
mcqs.explanation: Operational-Level Systems keep track of daily regular business transactions of the organisation.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is NOT a component of MIS structure?
mcqs.explanation: Lunchroom is NOT a component of MIS structure. The seven components are Hardware, Software, Database, Medium and Message, Input/Output, Operating Personnel and Procedure.

mcqs.q: In MIS structure, 'Procedure' refers to:
options: Rules and methods governing system operations
mcqs.explanation: In MIS structure, 'Procedure' refers to the rules and methods governing system operations.

mcqs.q: In MIS structure, 'Operating Personnel' refers to:
options: People who operate and manage the system
mcqs.explanation: Operating Personnel are the people who operate and manage the MIS.

mcqs.q: In MIS structure, 'Medium and Message' refers to:
options: Communication channels and the data transmitted
mcqs.explanation: In MIS structure, 'Medium and Message' refers to the communication channels and the data that is transmitted.

mcqs.q: Which of the following BEST describes the 'Database' component in MIS?
options: An organised collection of stored data
mcqs.explanation: In MIS structure, the Database is the organised collection of stored data on which the MIS operates.

mcqs.q: In MIS structure, 'Input and Output' refers to:
options: Data entered into and results produced by the system
mcqs.explanation: In MIS structure, Input and Output refer to the data entered into the system and the results produced by the system.

mcqs.q: SDLC stands for:
mcqs.explanation: SDLC stands for Software Development Life Cycle. The System Development process is also called the System Development Life Cycle.

mcqs.q: What is the first step in the SDLC?
mcqs.explanation: The first step in the SDLC is Problem / Opportunity Identification.

mcqs.q: Which is the most creative and challenging phase of the system life cycle?
mcqs.explanation: The most creative and challenging phase of the system life cycle is Design.

mcqs.q: What is the first phase of IT planning called?
mcqs.explanation: The first phase of IT planning is called Strategic Planning.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is NOT a phase of the software life cycle?
mcqs.explanation: Abstraction is NOT a phase of the software life cycle.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is NOT typically considered an attribute of software engineering?
options: Dependability — actually this IS an attribute; the source notes Dependability is NOT
mcqs.explanation: Per the source material, Dependability is NOT considered an attribute of software engineering. (Many modern texts include it; this MCQ follows the source convention.)

mcqs.q: CASE in software engineering stands for:
mcqs.explanation: CASE stands for Computer Aided Software Engineering — tools that support different stages of the software development life cycle.

mcqs.q: Which is considered the classical method of processing data?
mcqs.explanation: Batch Processing is considered the classical method of processing data — collecting transactions and processing them as a group.

mcqs.q: The Top-Down Approach is also known as:
mcqs.explanation: The Top-Down Approach (also known as stepwise design or decomposition) is essentially the breaking down of a system to gain insight into its compositional sub-systems in a reverse engineering fashion.

mcqs.q: The Prototyping Model is a system development method where:
options: Code is written first and tested last
A prototype is built, tested and reworked until acceptable, then the complete system is developed
mcqs.explanation: The Prototyping Model is a systems development method in which a prototype is built, tested and then reworked as necessary until an acceptable outcome is achieved, from which the complete system can be developed.

mcqs.q: What is one advantage of creating a prototype?
options: It increases the price of the project
It allows developers to experiment with different design options and serves as a means of communication between developers and customers
It removes the need for testing
It eliminates the need for users
mcqs.explanation: Advantages of a prototype include letting developers experiment with different design options and serving as a means of communication between developers and customers.

mcqs.q: EDI stands for:
mcqs.explanation: EDI stands for Electronic Data Interchange — the direct, computer-to-computer exchange of standardised business transaction documents between partners, suppliers and customers.

mcqs.q: EDP stands for:
mcqs.explanation: EDP stands for Electronic Data Processing — the use of computers to process data.

mcqs.q: SRS stands for:
mcqs.explanation: SRS stands for Software Requirements Specification — the document that describes the functional and non-functional requirements of the software.

mcqs.q: GIS in MIS stands for:
mcqs.explanation: GIS stands for Geographical Information System — a system that stores and manipulates geographically referenced data.

mcqs.q: DSS stands for:
mcqs.explanation: DSS stands for Decision Support System — supports structured and semi-structured decision making.

mcqs.q: ESS stands for:
mcqs.explanation: ESS stands for Executive Support System — software that transforms enterprise data into executive-level reports.

mcqs.q: What is the starting point of MIS planning?
mcqs.explanation: The starting point of MIS planning is general business planning — it should provide support for management planning, decision making and action.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is/are an information input characteristic of MIS?
options: Summary transaction data, high-volume data, and simple models
mcqs.explanation: Summary transaction data, high-volume data, and simple models are information inputs characteristic of MIS.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is/are known as Functional Business Areas?
options: Accounting, finance, marketing and human resources
mcqs.explanation: Accounting, finance, marketing and human resources are known as Functional Business Areas.

mcqs.q: What is the major drawback in decision making by MIS?
options: It always uses too much electricity
It is difficult due to rules for business problems
mcqs.explanation: The major drawback in decision making by MIS is that it is difficult due to rules for business problems.
